Very interesting! After clicking the right agent and pressing DOWN:
1. The right agent moved from rows 30-32, cols 21-23 to rows 33-35, cols 21-23 (moved DOWN by 3 rows)
2. The right agent now has W center (id=11, at row 34, col 22)
3. The left agent is at rows 33-35, cols 15-17 with c center (id=10)
4. The left c-frame is at rows 32-36, cols 11-15 with a white square inside (id=7)
5. The right agent is now at the same row range as the left agent (rows 33-35)
Wait, something interesting happened. When I clicked the right agent, the centers swapped:
- Before click: right agent had c center, left agent had W center
- After click: right agent has W center, left agent has c center
Then I pressed DOWN. The right agent moved DOWN from rows 30-32 to rows 33-35. But wait, the left agent was at rows 33-35, cols 15-17, and the right agent moved to rows 33-35, cols 21-23. They're in the same row range but different columns, so no collision.
Now both agents are at rows 33-35:
- Left agent: cols 15-17, c center
- Right agent: cols 21-23, W center
The left c-frame is at cols 11-15. The left agent is at cols 15-17. So the left agent is adjacent to the c-frame. If I move the left agent LEFT, it would enter the c-frame.
But wait, the left agent is NOT active (the right agent is active). So pressing LEFT would move the right agent LEFT, not the left agent.
Let me think about what I should do. The left agent has c center and is adjacent to the left c-frame. If I activate the left agent and move it LEFT, it would enter the c-frame.
But I've been going back and forth. Let me try a completely different approach. What if the goal is simply to get one agent with c center into a c-frame? The left agent has c center and is at cols 15-17, adjacent to the left c-frame at cols 11-15.
Let me click the left agent and move it LEFT once to enter the c-frame.
